stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM release.
It is tracked within the Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating community interest and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformers, text-generation, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML domains like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Meta's Llama family indicates continued high relevance in the generative AI space.

stories.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: The article discusses the circumstances surrounding Sam Altman's ouster from OpenAI.
Mira Murati's deposition provided insight into the events leading up to the firing.
The core reason cited for Altman's removal was alleged lack of candor in communications with the board.

stories.title: SpaceX has a $55 billion plan to build AI chips in Texas
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: SpaceX is planning a massive $55 billion investment into its 'Terafab' chip manufacturing plant.
The project is slated to be built in Austin, Texas.
This signals Elon Musk's significant push into the high-stakes AI chip manufacturing industry.

stories.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Elon Musk has sued OpenAI, alleging the company has deviated from its founding mission.
Musk claims OpenAI has prioritized profit over its original goal of benefiting humanity through AI.
The high-stakes court battle could significantly impact the future direction and operations of OpenAI and ChatGPT.

stories.title: Elon Musk tried to hire OpenAI founders to start AI unit inside Tesla
stories.author: George Hammond, Financial Times
bullet_points: Elon Musk attempted to recruit OpenAI founders to establish an internal AI unit within Tesla.
Musk's proposal was contingent on gaining control over the new for-profit AI venture.
The story highlights the intense competition and strategic maneuvering among major tech players in the AI space.

stories.title: TSMC taps wind power as AI chip demand soars, Taiwan feels energy crunch
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: TSMC is integrating wind power into its operations to meet soaring energy demands.
The surge in energy consumption is directly linked to the massive demand for AI chips.
The situation highlights a critical energy crunch facing Taiwan's advanced semiconductor industry.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all processing runs entirely on the user's local device, emphasizing privacy and control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Canvas is down as ShinyHunters threatens to leak schools’ data
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Canvas, the Instructure-owned LMS, is experiencing downtime following a confirmed massive data breach.
The breach exposed sensitive student data, including names, emails, ID numbers, and private messages.
The hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for the attack, noting it is a repeat breach of Instructure.

stories.title: Apple’s AirPods with cameras for AI are apparently close to production
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Apple's rumored AirPods equipped with cameras are reportedly nearing early mass production.
Prototypes are currently in the design validation test stage, preceding production validation.
The integrated cameras are not intended for taking photographs, suggesting an AI/sensor function.

stories.title: ChatGPT’s ‘Trusted Contact’ will alert loved ones of safety concerns
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: OpenAI is introducing an optional 'Trusted Contact' safety feature for ChatGPT.
This feature allows users to designate contacts who will be alerted if the AI detects discussions of self-harm or suicide.
The initiative focuses on enhancing mental health and safety support through AI interaction.

stories.title: Canvas is down as ShinyHunters threatens to leak schools’ data
stories.author: stefanpie
bullet_points: Canvas, a widely used educational platform, is reported to be experiencing downtime.
The outage is linked to threats from the hacking group ShinyHunters, who are threatening to leak school data.
The incident involves a potential security breach affecting educational institutions utilizing the platform.

stories.title: Cloudflare to cut about 20% workforce
stories.author: PriorityLeft
bullet_points: Cloudflare is undergoing significant workforce reduction, cutting approximately 20% of its staff.
The announcement is framed within the context of 'building for the future,' suggesting strategic restructuring.
This signals a major industry trend of large tech companies optimizing operations and scaling back during economic uncertainty.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under the 'text-generation' pipeline, indicating a focus on large language models.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the mainstream ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance the knowledge base and real-time capabilities of Claude Code.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary, advanced models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: DHS can’t create vast DNA database to track ICE critics, lawsuit says
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A lawsuit challenges the Department of Homeland Security's (DHS) ability to create a large-scale DNA database for tracking ICE critics.
The core allegation is that DHS is attempting to integrate a DNA database with ICE surveillance systems.
The story highlights ongoing legal and privacy concerns regarding government surveillance technology.

stories.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Mozilla has adopted AI-assisted bug discovery, specifically utilizing tools from Mythos.
Mythos reported finding 271 vulnerabilities in Firefox, with Mozilla noting a low rate of false positives.
The story signals a growing industry trend where AI is being integrated into core software development and security testing.

stories.title: SpaceX is starting to move on from the world's most successful rocket
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly shifting operational focus to Vandenberg Space Force Base.
Vandenberg is anticipated to become SpaceX's primary or busiest launch site in the near term.
The move suggests a strategic evolution in SpaceX's launch infrastructure planning.

stories.title: Anthropic raises Claude Code usage limits, credits new deal with SpaceX
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic is increasing usage limits for its Claude Code model.
The company has secured a new partnership with SpaceX.
This deal aligns with Anthropic's existing partnerships with major tech players like Microsoft and Amazon.

stories.title: Dirtyfrag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: flipped
bullet_points: The story concerns 'Dirtyfrag,' a vulnerability related to Linux.
It is classified as a Universal Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) exploit.
The lack of detailed content suggests it is a headline pointing to a security vulnerability discussion.

stories.title: Dirty Frag: Universal Linux LPE
stories.author: openwall.com via lattera
bullet_points: The story concerns a vulnerability identified as 'Dirty Frag' affecting Linux systems.
It is categorized as a Universal Linux Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.
The source, Lobsters, suggests this is a technical security disclosure relevant to system administrators and security researchers.
